intro: Raid Scout searches Twitch in a creator-controlled order, filters unsafe or repetitive destinations, and suggests one live channel. Suggest then Confirm is the default; no raid starts merely because discovery completed.

labels.suggest-only: Suggest only; never start a raid

sections.title: 1. Turn on Raid Scout
sections.description: Start here. Suggest then Confirm is the safest mode and keeps the final raid under your control.

sections.title: 2. Where to search
sections.description: Choose the Twitch sources Raid Scout may use and the order it should try them. It stops at the first source with eligible live channels.

sections.title: 3. Preferred channels
sections.description: Shown only when preferred-channel search is enabled. Paste one Twitch login per line or separate logins with commas.

sections.title: 4. Search limits
sections.description: Only limits for enabled search sources are shown. Conservative limits keep Twitch requests fast and bounded.

sections.title: 5. Audience size
sections.description: Keep suggestions within a comfortable viewer range. Similar-size matching reveals its ratio controls only when enabled.

sections.title: 6. Language, category, and tags
sections.description: Require a matching language when needed, then exclude categories or tags you do not want recommended.

sections.title: 7. Blocked channels and repeat protection
sections.description: Your own channel, offline channels, the current suggestion, blocked channels, and recent confirmed raids are always removed.

sections.title: 8. Confirmation and chat messages
sections.description: Set how long a suggestion remains valid and choose which outcomes should be announced in chat. Message boxes appear only when their announcement is enabled.

sections.title: 9. Dock and on-stream cards
sections.description: Use the add-on URL privately as a dock to review suggestions. Disable suggestion cards if an unconfirmed destination should not appear on-stream.

sections.title: 10. Card appearance
sections.description: Choose a readable card style for OBS, Meld, Streamlabs, or a private browser dock.

sections.title: Safety, controls, and privacy
sections.description: Use the imported Suggest, Confirm, and Cancel actions from a creator-controlled deck, hotkey, or operator command.
sections.notice: Raid Scout stores only bounded candidate summaries, shuffle bags, stream counters, and the last 100 suggestion/raid outcomes in private local state. Twitch authentication stays inside the triggerless Streamer.bot controller and is sent only to fixed api.twitch.tv endpoints. Tokens are never relayed, logged, saved by StreamBridge, or exposed to the overlay.
